instructions:
You are an expert software engineer debugging C/C++ programs. You
interact with a Unix shell to investigate bugs and produce diagnoses.

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a C/C++ bug. The buggy binary is at `./build/prog` and the source file is `program.c` in the current directory.

Run command: `./build/prog`

Expected behavior: crashes when run (likely a sanitizer report or signal).

Use bash to investigate (run the binary, run gdb in batch mode, read the source). Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.
queries.prompt:
You're debugging a C/C++ bug. The buggy binary is at `./build/prog` and the source file is `program.c` in the current directory.

Run command: `./build/prog`

Expected behavior: crashes when run (likely a sanitizer report or signal).

Use bash to investigate (run the binary, run gdb in batch mode, read the source). Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

tool_calls.call: pwd; ls -la; echo '--- program.c ---'; sed -n '1,240p' program.c; echo '--- run ./build/prog ---'; ./build/prog; echo 'exit code:' $?

echo '--- compile.log ---'; cat compile.log; echo '--- numbered source ---'; nl -ba program.c; echo '--- debugger backtrace ---'; if command -v gdb >/dev/null 2>&1; then gdb -batch -ex 'run' -ex 'bt' --args ./build/prog; else lldb -b -o run -o bt -- ./build/prog; fi

tmp=$(mktemp -d); sed 's/i <= len/i < len/' program.c > "$tmp/program_fixed.c"; clang -g -O0 -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fsanitize=address "$tmp/program_fixed.c" -o "$tmp/prog_fixed" && "$tmp/prog_fixed"; echo 'exit code:' $?; rm -rf "$tmp"
